questions.question: Where was the Maritime Financing Summit 2025 held?
questions.explanation: Recently, the Ministry of Ports, Shipping & Waterways hosted the Maritime Financing Summit 2025 in New Delhi. It supports the Maritime Amrit Kaal Vision (MAKV) 2047 to make India a top maritime power. Container capacity rose by over 70%, and cargo via coastal and inland waterways grew. India allows 100% Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in shipping and offers incentives via GIFT City IFSC. A Maritime Development Fund (MDF) was announced to boost long-term finance in shipbuilding and port infrastructure. Parliament passed the Bills of Lading Bill 2025 to simplify shipping document laws.

questions.question: Which space organization has launched a twin satellite mission called “TRACERS” to study Earth’s magnetic shield?
questions.explanation: Recently, NASA launched a twin satellite mission called TRACERS, which stands for Tandem Reconnection and Cusp Electrodynamics Reconnaissance Satellites. The satellites were launched from Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket. The mission aims to study magnetic reconnection, a powerful process where magnetic field lines snap and release energy. This process happens when solar wind, a stream of charged particles from the Sun, interacts with Earth’s magnetosphere (magnetic shield).

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the new Chairman of the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) in July 2025?
questions.explanation: Recently, on July 24, 2025, the government appointed Ajay Seth as the new Chairman of the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI). The appointment was approved by the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet. Ajay Seth is a 1987-batch Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officer from the Karnataka cadre. He retired as Union Finance and Economic Affairs Secretary in June 2025. He will serve as IRDAI Chairman for three years or till the age of 65 or until further orders, whichever comes earlier. He succeeds Debasish Panda, also a former IAS officer, whose term ended in March 2025.

questions.question: The Scheme for Promotion of Culture of Science (SPoCS) is an initiative of which ministry?
questions.explanation: Recently, Union Minister for Culture and Tourism Gajendra Singh Shekhawat shared updates on Scheme for Promotion of Culture of Science (SPoCS) in Rajya Sabha. Scheme for Promotion of Culture of Science (SPoCS) is a flagship scheme of the Ministry of Culture, Government of India. It aims to foster scientific temper and awareness among the public and students. National Council of Science Museums (NCSM) is the nodal agency for implementation. SPoCS supports Science Cities, Science Centres, Innovation Hubs, and Digital Planetariums. As of July 2025, 27 Science Centres have been set up across India. These centres offer hands-on exhibits, workshops, and outreach to promote science.

questions.question: What is the rank of India in the Numbeo Safety Index 2025?
questions.explanation: Recently, the Numbeo Safety Index 2025 ranked India at 67th with a safety score of 55.8. The Numbeo Safety Index is a real-time, user-based global ranking that reflects how safe people feel in their own countries. It considers factors like crime fear, crime incidents, and overall public safety perceptions. In 2025, the index covered 146 countries worldwide. Top countries in safety include Andorra (84.7), United Arab Emirates–UAE (84.5), Qatar (84.2), Taiwan (82.9), and Oman (81.7). These countries maintain high safety through strong law enforcement, political stability, and safety policies.

questions.question: Where was India’s first hydrogen-powered coach successfully tested?
questions.explanation: Recently, Indian Railways successfully tested India’s first hydrogen-powered coach at Integral Coach Factory (ICF), Chennai. This marks a major step in India’s shift towards clean and green transportation. The tested coach is a 1,200 Horse Power (HP) hydrogen train under development. It is part of the Hydrogen for Heritage initiative to run 35 hydrogen-powered trains on heritage and hill routes. Each train will cost ₹80 crore, with ₹70 crore for supporting ground infrastructure per route. Hydrogen fuel offers zero carbon emissions, supporting India’s clean energy transition.

questions.question: Atal Pension Yojana is regulated by which organization?

options.label: C
options.text: Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)

options.label: D
options.text: Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA)

answer.label: D
answer.text: Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA)

questions.explanation: Atal Pension Yojana (APY) has crossed 8 crore enrolments in 2025, marking its 10th anniversary. 39 lakh new subscribers were added in the current financial year. APY is a government-backed pension scheme launched in 2015. It provides social security to workers in the unorganised sector. It is regulated by the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA). The scheme guarantees fixed monthly pension after the age of 60. It promotes retirement planning for people without formal pension coverage. It supports inclusive financial security and long-term welfare for low-income workers.

questions.question: Where was the first-ever Armed Forces National Conference, SHAPE 2025 organized?
questions.explanation: The Department of Hospital Administration at Army Hospital Research & Referral (AHRR), New Delhi, recently organised SHAPE 2025, the first-ever Armed Forces National Conference on Sustainable Hospital Architecture, Planning, Infrastructure and Equipment, on July 26–27, 2025. SHAPE 2025 aligned with the Viksit Bharat@2047 vision, marking a step toward future-ready and resilient healthcare infrastructure. The conference stressed the shift from traditional hospital design to environmentally conscious, technology-enabled, and patient-friendly planning. It called sustainability a strategic need for health equity, disaster readiness, and national preparedness.

questions.question: Where was India’s first grassland bird census conducted using the Acoustic Monitoring Technique?
questions.explanation: Recently, Prime Minister Narendra Modi praised the first-ever grassland bird census in Kaziranga National Park, Assam, during the 124th episode of his radio show ‘Mann Ki Baat’. The census identified 43 bird species, including many rare and endangered ones. The census used the Acoustic Monitoring Technique to study rare and threatened bird species. Sound recording devices were installed and analysed on computers to identify bird species by their calls without disturbing them. The initiative shows how technology and human effort can support biodiversity conservation.

questions.question: World Day Against Trafficking in Persons is observed on which day every year?
questions.explanation: World Day Against Trafficking in Persons is observed every year on July 30. It aims to raise awareness about human trafficking and protect victims’ rights. It was established by a United Nations (UN) resolution and first observed in 2014. In the United States, National Human Trafficking Awareness Day is marked on January 11. This U.S. observance was established in 2007 to spread awareness about trafficking and modern slavery. The theme for 2025 is “Human Trafficking is Organized Crime – End the Exploitation”. The day highlights the need for global action to combat trafficking networks and support survivors.
